title: How Patterns Dictate Learnability in Sequential Data

repos.name: EkMeasurable__Learnability_Ipred
repos.evidence: Paper text line 74 states 'The codes are available on GitHub: https://github.com/EkMeasurable/Learnability_Ipred'. README title is 'How Patterns Dictate Learnability in Sequential Data' — exact paper title match. Repo contains the paper's own estimators (utils/estimators.py, utils/lower_bounds.py implementing SMILE/NWJ/InfoNCE/DV/TUBA), experiment scripts (experiments/experiment_1.py for Gaussian process Ipred estimation, experiments/experiment_2.py for Ising-spin learning curves), and committed result artifacts (results_data/spin_xps/*.json, results_data/ar_sequence_estimation_*/) that the auditor compared against paper tables.

rationale: The single cloned repo is unambiguously this paper's own implementation: the paper's text includes an explicit GitHub URL matching the repo (https://github.com/EkMeasurable/Learnability_Ipred), the README title matches the paper title exactly, and the code structure (Ipred estimators, universal learning curve computation, AR and Ising-spin experiments) directly implements the paper's contributions. All audit findings were correctly written against this core repo. No non-core repos were cloned.
audit_impact: None. The audit correctly identified and evaluated the paper's own code. All 11 findings (missing, bug, difference, methodology categories) are legitimately written against the core repo. No findings were misdirected at a baseline or dependency.
recommended_action: No provenance correction needed. The audit's findings stand as written.
